assertion_results.text: Each task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements

Task 1 (advisory-severity-model): Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Create, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Task 2 (advisory-severity-service): Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. assertion_results.text: Task count is between 3 and 10 inclusive
assertion_results.evidence: 5 task files in outputs directory: task-1-advisory-severity-model.md, task-2-advisory-severity-service.md, task-3-advisory-summary-endpoint.md, task-4-cache-invalidation.md, task-5-integration-tests.md. 5 is between 3 and 10 inclusive.
All 5 task files have '## Repository\ntrustify-backend' as the first section. Verified in task-1 (line 2), task-2 (line 2), task-3 (line 2), task-4 (line 2), task-5 (line 2).

assertion_results.text: Task dependencies form a DAG with no circular dependencies
assertion_results.evidence: Dependency graph: Task 1 has no dependencies. Task 2 depends on Task 1. Task 3 depends on Task 1 and Task 2. Task 4 depends on Task 3. Task 5 depends on Task 3. This forms a DAG: 1 → 2 → 3 → {4, 5}. No cycles exist — all dependency edges point from lower to higher task numbers.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 task files end with a properly formatted digest line: Task 1: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:c3c8e504572dee406c9d7628939287b25cc4271ed4d18a7390f5e3d45ff076f3' (64 hex chars verified). Task 2: 'sha256-md:3fb4b737709a6139a16c5115a3adc2b9a42c0679cd4b6e989092eb80fed5ddaf' (64 hex chars). Task 3: 'sha256-md:8f872cd867e33c7db517f1a35e795d00d70f32695457ddd5f4a717a2d587ddae' (64 hex chars). Task 4: 'sha256-md:37c9d3772ada872ca0e0869af127f069798cdb8657f8cb173cf08ca22bf4dc4b' (64 hex chars). Task 5: 'sha256-md:cbdd01bc473eb39b2af40b06a5dad07c508d9048fc176306dfcaf22a81230694' (64 hex chars). All are exactly 64 lowercase hex characters, not placeholders or abbreviated values.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 tasks include only applicable conventions with proper rationale format. No task lists any convention with 'Not applicable', 'N/A', or 'Excluded' annotations. Rationale examples: Task 1: 'Applies: task creates modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/advisory_summary.rs matching the convention's module pattern scope.' Task 2: 'Applies: task modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs matching the convention's .rs file scope.' and 'Applies: convention has no file-type restriction (broadly applicable).' (correct format for unscoped conventions per convention-applicability-rules.md). Task 3: 'Applies: task modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs matching the convention's .rs file scope.' Task 4: 'Applies: task modifies modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs matching the convention's .rs file scope.' Task 5: 'Applies: task creates tests/api/sbom_advisory_summary.rs matching the convention's .rs file scope.' All rationales follow the prescribed format, not free-form prose. Inapplicable conventions (e.g., Response types/PaginatedResults not in model tasks, Migration Patterns) are correctly excluded entirely.
